data.title: Adroit Infotech Says Rights Issue Proceeds Used as Planned
data.description:
Adroit Infotech (, NSE:ADROITPP) said there was no deviation or variation in the use of proceeds from its 457.2 million-rupee rights issue completed on Feb. 14, according to a statement submitted to Indian stock exchanges.

The funds were allocated toward business expansion, repayment of non-current liabilities, working capital needs, general corporate purposes, and issue-related expenses, the company said.

A portion earmarked for acquisitions remains unutilized and is held in an account with Axis Bank.

data.title: Philippine cbank says it has room for two more rate cuts in 2025
data.description:
There is room to cut Philippine interest rates by 25 basis points twice more this year, but they will not necessarily be at consecutive meetings and the central bank has to be careful not to cut by too much, Governor Eli Remolona said on Friday.

Inflation should remain low in coming months, with growth expected to moderate this year and next, he told a briefing, adding he expected the current economic uncertainty to persist.

"We still have to be careful because we don't want to cut too much," Remolona told reporters.

The central bank resumed its easing cycle last month, cutting its key policy rate (PHCBIR=ECI) by 25 basis points to 5.5%. The next policy meeting is scheduled for June 19.

Annual inflation slowed to 1.4% in April, the lowest rate since November 2019. This brought the year-to-date average inflation to 2.0%, the bottom of the central bank's 2.0% to 4.0% target range for the year.

data.title: Silk Logistics Holdings Says ACCC Resumes Review of Proposed Acquisition by DP World Australia
data.description:
Silk Logistics Holdings said that the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission resumed its review regarding the proposed acquisition of all of the shares in the firm by DP World Australia via a scheme of arrangement, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

The revised date for announcement of the regulator's findings is July 10. The scheme meeting is currently schedule for June 20. The end date under the scheme implementation deed is June 30.

The firm is reviewing its position regarding the revised ACCC decision date and is in talks with DP World Australia.

Silk Logistics said that it has not issued a notice to extend the end date under the scheme and it has not received such a notice from DP World.

data.title: Luharuka Media Renamed DhanSafal Finserve to Reflect Financial Services Focus
data.description:
Luharuka Media & Infra Ltd has rebranded as DhanSafal Finserve Ltd, marking its shift toward financial services, the company said in a local filing on Thursday.

DhanSafal will roll out its new identity across platforms in the coming weeks, including a revised stock ticker.

data.title: Indus Towers to Buy 26% Stake in JSW Green Energy Eight SPV for INR380 Million
data.description:
Indus Towers will acquire a 26% stake in JSW Green Energy Eight, a special purpose vehicle for solar power, for about 380.4 million Indian rupees in cash, according to a Thursday filing on the local bourses.

The telecom infrastructure company said the move supports its renewable energy procurement under a captive model and aligns with its net zero goals.

data.title: Channel Infrastructure Sees Higher 2025 Dividend; Shares Near 6.5-Year High
data.description:
Channel Infrastructure NZ expects 2025 dividend in the lower end of the policy range of NZ$0.12 to NZ$0.125, up from NZ$0.11 in 2024, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The board also increased its dividend payout ratio in the range of 70% to 90% of normalised free cash flow, up from its previous policy of 60% to 70%, the filing said.

The company reaffirmed its 2025 earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization guidance in the range of NZ$89 million to NZ$94 million, the filing added.

CHI also said it will introduce a dividend reinvestment plan and consider a dual Australian Stock Exchange listing.

Shares of the company rose past 3% in recent Friday trade, hitting their highest since February 2019.

data.title: Japan's super-long yields hover near record highs on inflation, fiscal concerns
data.description:
Japanese government bonds remained stable on Friday, after a volatile week in which super-long yields hit record highs as inflation and fiscal concerns sapped demand for debt.

Yields on 20-, 30-, and 40-year Japanese government bonds have been climbing, fuelled by growing concerns over the country's worsening fiscal health, as some political parties advocate for consumption tax cuts to counter rising prices.

Data on Friday showed Japan's core consumer inflation hit 3.5% in April, its fastest annual pace in more than two years, and keeping pressure on the Bank of Japan to continue raising interest rates.

A weak auction of 20-year bonds on Tuesday underscored the market's diminishing capacity to absorb new debt needed to finance the government's fiscal deficit.

BOJ Governor Kazuo Ueda said on Thursday that the central bank will closely monitor market developments, as yields on super-long JGBs climbed to record highs this week.

"The risk of JGBs becoming 'indigestible' in the ultra-long term zone remains," Mizuho analysts said in a research note on Friday. "The incentive to reduce issuance of super-long bonds (thereby shortening the duration) is relatively strong."

The 30-year JGB yield (JP30YTN=JBTC) rose 1 basis point to 3.175%, hovering near an all-time high of 3.185% hit on Wednesday. The 40-year yield slid half a basis point to 3.665%, edging lower from the record 3.675% touched on Thursday.

The benchmark 10-year JGB yield (JP10YTN=JBTC) fell 0.5 basis point to 1.555%. The two-year yield (JP2YTN=JBTC) rose 0.5 basis point to 0.735%, and five-year yield (JP5YTN=JBTC) was flat at 1.03%.

data.title: Hengrui Pharma Surges 29% in Hong Kong Debut
data.description:
Jiangsu Hengrui Pharmaceuticals , made a stellar trading debut in Hong Kong Friday morning as its shares soared over 29% at the opening bell.

The Chinese drugmaker opened at HK$57 per share, well above its IPO price of HK$44.05.

data.title: Dateline Resources Starts Bankable Feasibility Study for California Project; Shares Reach Over Two-Year High
data.description:
Dateline Resources started the bankable feasibility study for its Colosseum project in California, based on the October 2024 project definition study, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The company adopted a long-term gold price forecast of $2,900 per ounce for the study, combining recent price performance with a consensus long-term outlook, the filing said.

The study is exploring potential gold price increases to boost production and value by lowering the cut-off grade, according to the filing.

Shares rose 4% in morning trade Friday, reaching their highest since December 2022.
